title: Deparia subfluvialis
intro: Deparia subfluvialis, also known as Himalayan golden-fern or Himalayan chain fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Deparia subfluvialis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Deparia subfluvialis is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Deparia subfluvialis is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Deparia, which consists of approximately 60 species of ferns. This genus is a part of the larger group of ferns known as the Osmundales, which includes about 2,000 species of ferns worldwide. Deparia subfluvialis is native to East Asia, specifically in countries such as China, Japan, and Korea. The classification of Deparia subfluvialis is closely related to that of other species within the Athyriaceae family, which is characterized by their unique fern-like fronds.
uses: Deparia subfluvialis is reported to have medicinal properties, with its leaves used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is said to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. Additionally, Deparia subfluvialis is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal uses.

These compounds are found in various parts of the plant and have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in Deparia subfluvialis have been identified as kaempferol and quercetin derivatives, which are known for their potential health benefits.
